title: Fig. 6 in Protozoal encephalitis associated with Sarcocystis calchasi and S. falcatula during an epizootic involving Brandt's cormorants (Phalacrocorax penicillatus) in coastal Southern California, USA
author: Bamac, Ozge Erdogan
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Fig. 6. Gel electrophoresis of the S. calchasi ITS1 PCR of the brain (B) and muscle (M) tissues from eight Brandt's cormorants with neurologic disease and protozoal encephalitis. Bird Nos. 1–7 from Los Angeles County are strongly positive (No. 1 muscle tissue is negative likely due to sampling and too low quantity) while No. 8 from Orange County is negative on all tissues. The encephalitis in bird 8 was demonstrated to be due to S. falcatula. Columns C1M, C2M, C3M and C3B are positive control tissues from pigeons with S. calchasi infection.
